knsm-eiland.e: Compound of the name of the Koninklijke Nederlandse Stoomboot-Maatschappij ("Royal Dutch Steamboat Company") and eiland (“island”). The area formerly housed the company's shipyards.

s.def: a village and former municipality of Waterland, North Holland, Netherlands

katwoude.e: Attested as kattwoude in 1310. Compound of the element kat (“cat, lesser, arched ridge, mud”) and Middle Dutch wout (“marsh forest, swamp forest”).

kwadijk.e: First attested as coedijc in 1393. Compound of Middle Dutch coe (“cow”) and dyc (“levee”). The first element was gradually replaced with quaet (“in poor condition”).

katham.e: Attested as Katham in 1665. Compound of the element kat (“cat, lesser, arched ridge, mud”) and Middle Dutch hamme (“alluvial land in the bend of a watercourse”). The first element may actually be a variant of kade (“quay, embankment”). Compare Kathoek.

klaterbuurt.e: Attested as De Clater-buyrt in 1654. Compound of klater (“mushy chunk of mud”) and buurt (“hamlet, small settlement”). Compare Kladde, Klatering, Kletterbuurt, Kleuter and Klundert.

kogerpolder.e: Compound of koog (“land located outside of a levee”) and polder (“polder”). Named after a polder.

a village and former municipality of Zaanstad, North Holland, Netherlands

koog aan de zaan.e: Attested as upte Koech in 1561. Derived from koog (“land located outside a levee”) and the hydronym Zaan.

s.def: a village and former municipality of Zaanstad, North Holland, Netherlands

krommenie.e: First attested as crommenye in 1276. Derived from the hydronym Krommenie, derived in turn from Middle Dutch crom (“bent”) and a variant of aa (“watercourse, river”).

s.def: a hamlet in Zaanstad, North Holland, Netherlands

krommeniedijk.e: First attested as crommenier-dijck in 1449. Compound of the toponym Krommenie and dijk (“levee”).

kogerveld.e: Compound of the toponym Koog aan de Zaan and veld (“field”).

kunstschildersbuurt.e: Compound of the plural form of kunstschilder (“painter”) and buurt (“neighbourhood, quarter”). The neighbourhood's streets are named after famous painters.

s.def: a neighbourhood of Haarlem, North Holland, Netherlands

karolingenbuurt.e: Compound of the plural form of Karolinger (“Carolingian”) and buurt (“neighbourhood, quarter”). The neighbourhood's streets are named after members of the Carolingian Dynasty.

s.def: a neighbourhood of Haarlem, North Holland, Netherlands

kruistochtbuurt.e: Compound of kruistocht (“crusade”) and buurt (“neighbourhood, quarter”). The neighbourhood's streets are named after places and people of the Crusades.

s.def: a neighbourhood of Haarlem, North Holland, Netherlands

koninginnebuurt.e: Compound of koningin (“queen”) and buurt (“neighbourhood, quarter”). The neighbourhood's streets are named after queens of the Netherlands.

kerkbeek.e: Attested as De Kerck Beeck in 1680. Compound of kerk (“church”) and beek (“brook, stream”). Named after a former brook which ran through Heemskerk.

klein dorregeest.e: Attested as thorengest in the 12th century. Compound of Old Dutch thoren (“thorn”) and gēst (“elevated sandy land”). The element klein (“small, lesser”) was added to distinguish the settlement from Groot Dorregeest.

a hamlet in Koggenland, North Holland, Netherlands

kathoek.e: Attested as d'Cathoeck in 1680. Potentially a compound of kade (“quay, embankment”) and hoek (“corner, protruding bend”). Compare Katham.

koggenland.e: Compound of the plural form of koog (“land located outside of a levee”) and land (“land”).

kersenboogerd.e: Compound of the plural form of kers (“cherry”) and a variant of bongerd (“orchard”).

kraaienburg.e: Attested as Kraaijenburg in 1886. Compound of the plural form of kraai (“crow, Corvus corone”) and burg (“manor”). Originally the name of a farmhouse.

koppershorn.e: Attested as Coppershorn in 1680. Compound of kopper (“worker who cuts off the heads of cod”) and horn (“corner, protruding bend”).

kolhorn.e: Attested as Kolhoiren in 1518. Potentially a compound of kol (“elevated place, top, head”) and horn (“protruding bend, corner”).

kreileroord.e: Attested as Dorp IV in 1930. Compound of the toponym Creil and oord (“place”). The name was chosen as the winner of a naming competition in the beginning of the 1950s. Compare Kreil.

s.def: a hamlet in Hollands Kroon, North Holland, Netherlands

kleine sluis.e: Attested as Kleine Sluis in 1839-1859. Derived from klein (“small”) and sluis (“lock, sluice”).

kreil.e: Attested as De Creyl in 1680. Derived from West Frisian kreil (“wild brushwood, brushwood growing on low-lying swampland”). See also Creil and Kreileroord.

keinse.e: First attested as kaense in 1319. Etymology unclear. Potentially derived from keen (“gully, creek”) suffixed with a collectivising suffix, although the morphology of the toponym does not match well with this etymology. Compare Keent and Kinrooi.

s.def: a village in Schagen, North Holland, Netherlands

krabbendam.e: First attested as crabbendamme in 1413-1414. Potentially formed analogously to krabbendijk (“sea defence consisting of wooden poles located in front of the main levee”). Compare Krabbendijke.

keinsmerbrug.e: First attested as Keijnsemer Brug in 1680. Compound of the toponym Keinse and brug (“bridge”).

klein maarslag.e: First attested as marisfliete in 944. Derived from klein (“small, lesser”) and a compound of Old Frisian mar (“lake, pool, watercourse”) and fliet (“tidal watercourse, river”). The second element was gradually reinterpreted to Dutch Low Saxon slacht (“levee”). The element klein was added to disti...

koenraadswetering.e: Attested as Coenraads Wetering in 1865. Compound of Coenraad (a surname) and wetering (“drainage channel”). Named after a nearby watercourse.

kersbergen.e: First attested as hoffstede geheyten Kerssberch in 1534. Compound of kers (“cherry”) and berg (“hill, elevated place”). Originally the name of a homestead.

kerckebosch.e: Compound of kerk (“church”) and bos (“forest”). Named after a manor built on land formerly owned by the Catholic church of Rijsenburg.

kattenbroek.e:
First attested as Cattenbroek in 1695. Compound of kat (“infertile”) and broek (“marsh, marshland”).
Compare Cattenbroek.

koerheuvel.e: Compound of Middle Dutch coer (“small watchtower, lookout”) and heuvel (“hill”). The hill may have been part of a system of observation points.

s.def: a neighbourhood of IJsselstein, Utrecht, Netherlands

kasteelkwartier.e: Compound of kasteel (“castle”) and kwartier (“quarter, neighbourhood”). Named after the former castle of IJsselstein.

s.def: a village and former municipality of Vijfheerenlanden, Utrecht, Netherlands

kedichem.e: First attested as kedinghen in 1395-1396. Compound of Old Dutch Kedo (a personal name) suffixed with the collectivising suffix -ing- and hēm (“home, settlement”).

kortenhoeven.e: First attested as cortenhover around 1343. Compound of Middle Dutch cort (“short”) and oever (“bank”). The second element was gradually reinterpreted to the plural form of hoeve (“piece of land of a certain size”).

knollemanshoek.e: First attested as snodelhoet [read: snodelhoec] in 1319. Originally a compound of Middle Dutch snodel (“dummy, fool”) and hoec (“isolated place”). The current toponym is a compound of Knolleman (a surname) and hoek. Compare Snel in Snel en Polanen, Snelleveld and Snijdelwijk.

kanis.e:
Attested as de Verkeerde Kanis herbg in 1839-1859. Derived from dialectal kanis (“covered fish basket”). Originally the name of an inn which used an upturned fish basket on its signage.
Compare Moespot, De Pan and Vuilpan.

s.def: a hamlet in Woerden, Utrecht, Netherlands

kromwijk.e: First attested as crumwijc around 1317. Compound of Middle Dutch crom (“crooked, bent”) and either wijc (“homestead, village, settlement”) or wijc (“inlet, side channel”).

kamerik-houtdijken.e: Compound of the toponyms Kamerik and Houtdijken. Originally the name of a legal district.

kamerik-mijzijde.e: Compound of the toponyms Kamerik and Mijzijde. Originally the name of a legal district.

kromme mijdrecht.e: Attested as Kromme-Mijdrecht in 1846. Named after a nearby watercourse, the name of which derives from krom (“crooked, bent”) and a second element for which see Mijdrecht.

kortrijk.e:
First attested as kurtryke and kortrike in 1217. Derived from Middle Dutch Kortrike (“Courtrai”), from earlier Medieval Latin forms Curtraco, Curtriaco, Corturiacensis, as old as Late Latin Cortoriacenses around 400.
Seemingly ultimately of Celtic origin, from the name *Kortoriako(n) with the com...

kanaleneiland.e: Compound of the plural form of kanaal (“canal”) and eiland (“island”). The neighbourhood was named for its location between two large canals.

klein engendaal.e: First attested as Engedaal in 1839-1859. Derived from klein (“small, little”) and a compound of the toponym De Eng and dal (“dale, valley”). Named after a former farmhouse.

a village and municipality of Belgium

kinrooi.e:
First attested as kynre in 1345. Potentially derived from a compound of dialectal keen (“pine tree, Pinus sp.”) and Middle Dutch rode (“land cleared of trees”). Compare Keent and Keinse.
See also Limburgish Kinder.

s.def: a village and former municipality of Kinrooi, Belgium

kessenich.e:
First attested as kesnic in 1102. Derived from Latin Cassinius (a personal name) suffixed with the Gallo-Roman suffix -acum.
See also Limburgish Kèsing, Kaesing.

s.def: a village and former municipality of Bocholt, Belgium

kaulille.e: First attested as caulill in 1259. Etymology uncertain.

kaviaar.e: From Ottoman Turkish خاویار, from Persian خاویار.

kapasie.e: From Sranan Tongo kapasi, from Kari'na kapasi.

krombaanvuurmond.e: Compound of krom (“curved, bent”) + baan (“trajectory, orbit”) + vuurmond (“cannon, gun”).

krombaangeschut.e: Compound of krom (“curved, bent”) + baan (“trajectory, orbit”) + geschut (“artillery”).

examples:

O muze, laat vooral mijn fiere moed niet zinken

knalpijp.e: From knal or knallen (“to bang, to make a loud, sharp noise”) + pijp (“pipe”).

KARMOZIJNROOD WEESKIND _(CATOCALA SPONSA)

of, from or pertaining to Cologne

keuls.e: From Middle Dutch coolsch. Equivalent to Keulen + -s.

kerspel.e: From Middle Dutch kerspel, from kerke (church) + spellen (to proclaim).

s.def: cog-like, resembling a cog (ship type of the High Middle Ages)
crab mentality (the tendency to thwart a successful person out of envy)
examples: De heer Ong-A-Kwie denkt hierbij aan het aantrekken van buitenlandse industrieën en aan het kans geven aan het ontwikkelen van eigen initiatieven. Volgens hem is de tijd voorbij van de zogenaamde krabben-mentaliteit. We moeten — volgens hem — niet als krabben in een ton elkaar verhinderen daaruit te komen. Als er iemand in slaagt naar boven te klauteren zodat hij uit die opgesloten situatie geraakt, dan moeten wij ons juist door die persoon laten meeslepen; dat is beter dan de betreffende persoon terug te trekken in de ton.

s.def: to babble, speak an unknown language

kwebbelkous.e: From kwebbelen (“to babble”) + kous (“stocking”); compare babbelkous.

s.def: the western cattle egret, Bubulcus ibis

koereiger.e: From koe (“cow”) + reiger (“heron”). So named because it is often seen alongside cattle at pasture.

s.def: any reed warbler (any bird of the genus Acrocephalus; not necessarily Acrocephalus scirpaceus)

examples: Zelf wist hij lange tijd helemaal niet dat hij "best goed" kan zingen. Tijdens het afscheid van groep 8, een jaar of drie geleden, moest hij karaoken.

kramsvogel.e: Early modern or late medieval borrowing from German Krammetsvogel.

kantoortijger.e: Compound of kantoor + tijger (“enthusiastic, motivated person”). Compare vergadertijger.

a crossbill: any bird in the genus Loxia

examples: Een kalenderjaar bestaat uit 12 maanden.
Het kalenderjaar begint op 1 januari.

katuil.e: From kat (“cat”) + uil (“owl”), used until the early 20th century.

klappen als een ekster.e: Literally, “to chatter like a magpie”. Attested from at least the 17th century, seems to have fallen out of use by the 20th century. Compare French bavard comme une pie.

s.def: Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see klein, waterhoen.

knieval.e: From knie + val. Compare German Kniefall.

kortsnavelraafkaketoe.e: From kort (“short”) + snavel (“beak”) + raafkaketoe (“black cockatoo”), contrasted with the langsnavelraafkaketoe.

kleine burgemeester.e: Literally, “small mayor”; compare grote burgemeester (“Larus hyperboreus”).

examples: De bos bloemen kwam koefnoen met de aankoop.

Coordinate term: kwalitatief

Uit de reacties van fabrikanten, supermarkten en de brancheverenigingen blijkt niet dat zij van plan zijn hiermee te stoppen. Wij blijven daarom oproepen om voorbeelden van krimpflatie te melden op ons meldpunt.

De Republiek kende bijvoorbeeld de 'verkleining der kannen' een maatregel die je in de kroeg niet meteen zou merken. Voor hetzelfde geld kreeg je minder bier. Dit noemt men tegenwoordig krimpflatie. Zo verdoezelde men een hogere accijns.

krimpflatie.e: Blend of krimpen (“to shrink”) + inflatie, a calque of English shrinkflation.

s.def: wood sorrel (any plant of the genus Oxalis)
a cliff swallow, any of various birds of the genus Petrochelidon that nest in cliffs

kruipertje.e: Diminutive, from kruiper (“crawler”) + -tje. The plant is so called because its pointy seedhead sticks to clothing easily and will slowly move over time while doing so.

s.def: any plant of the (former) genus Lychnis, now generally included in the genus Silene

koekoeksbloem.e: From koekoek (“cuckoo”) + -s- + bloem (“flower”). Various theories have been proposed for the name, including that it is named after the cuckoo because the cuckoo's song is first heard again around the same time these flowers bloom in spring (which is not necessarily exactly true), or that it is ...

s.def: a small hut for watching wildlife, preferably unnoticed
Hungarian mullein or showy mullein (Verbascum speciosum)
someone who is broke; a poor person

kuierlat.e: From kuieren (“to walk leisurely”) + lat (“slate, ruler”).

s.def: to overcome, to subdue; to break
examples: niet klein te krijgen
iemand klein krijgen

klein krijgen.e: Literally, “to get someone small”. Compare German kleinkriegen.

s.def: a tadpole person, headfooter: a primitive human figure drawn with the legs directly attached to the head, especially as drawn by small children

kopvoeter.e: From kop (“head”) + voet (“foot”) + -er. Compare German Kopffüßler and rare English headfooter.

examples: Er is een grote klimwand in het sportcentrum.
Ze heeft een klimwand in haar achtertuin.
De sportschool heeft een nieuwe klimmuur geïnstalleerd.
Kinderen vinden het leuk om de klimmuur in het park te gebruiken.
De kinderen spelen op het klimrek in de speeltuin.
Hij heeft een klimrek in zijn achtertuin voor zijn kinderen.

s.def: to act, in particular to talk, in a way considered overly devout
to talk nonsense, to say meaningless things

kwezelen.e: Probably onomatopoeic. Compare Middle Dutch queteren.

klimopbremraap.e: From klimop (“ivy”) + bremraap (“broomrape”); the species parasitizes on ivy.

kleine plevier.e: Literally, “small plover”, owing its name to the fact that it is the smallest species of plover typically found in the Low Countries.

s.def: to make no sense, be nonsensical
examples: Zijn redenering raakte kant noch wal.

s.def: little-robin (Geranium purpureum or Geranium robertianum subsp. purpureum)

klein robertskruid.e: Literally, “small herb Robert”; the species (or subspecies, according to some) very closely resembles robertskruid, Geranium robertianum.

examples:
15 Daarom is dit ook een kwaad, dat krankheid aanbrengt; dat hij in alle manier, gelijk hij gekomen is, alzo heengaat; en wat voordeel is het hem, dat hij in den wind gearbeid heeft?
16 Dat hij ook alle dagen in duisternis gegeten heeft; en dat hij veel verdriets gehad heeft, ook zijn krankheid, en onstuimigen toorn?

knopherik.e: Compound of knop (“bud, button”) + herik (“charlock mustard”).

s.def: a teasel, any plant of genus Dipsacus
a cross-shaped Gothic ornament or finial
a crucifer; any plant of the family Brassicaceae
synonym of vleugeltjesbloem (“milkwort, plant of genus Polygala”)
common restharrow (Ononis repens, syns. Ononis spinosa subsp. procurrens, Ononis spinosa subsp. repens

kruipend stalkruid.e: Literally, “creeping restharrow”; the plant tends to grow close to the ground instead of vertically upwards.

s.def: a loosestrife, any plant of the genus Lythrum
a birthday party where those in attendance mostly sit in a circle (sometimes two or more circles) and chit-chat, subjectively lacking exuberance or festiveness
imperial circle (administrative grouping of the Holy Roman Empire)
examples: Arnade begint zijn boek bij de Bourgondiërs, met hun ideaal van een door eigen hertogen geleide bestuurlijke eenheid die van Autun tot ongeveer Dokkum zou reiken. Dat ideaal lieten ze niet verdampen toen ze bij de Habsburgers ingetrouwd raakten (of de Habsburgers bij hen): keizer Karel V had rond 1550 iets op papier dat de facto misschien niet veel voorstelde, maar wel een ‘Bourgondische Kreits’ mocht heten – een ‘administratie’ waartegen met name Friezen en Geldersen zich altijd zijn blijven verzetten.

kreits.e: From Rhine Franconian kreytz, compare German Kreis.

s.def: a human head, particularly the brain
cruising speed (speed at which a vehicle normally moves when traveling long distances)
stride (pace at which one feels comfortable and things are going smoothly)

kruissnelheid.e: From kruisen + snelheid. Compare French vitesse de croisière.

s.def: contender, strong candidate (someone who has a good shot at winning)

kutwijf.e: From kut- (“derogatory prefix”) + wijf (“woman, broad, bitch”).

examples: Hij zag de kwajongens het bushokje kapotslaan.

s.def: to beat up, to beat to a pulp (give a severe beating)
examples: Als je me aanraakt komt mijn vader je helemaal kapotslaan.
Het bootje is door de zware branding kapotgeslagen.

s.def: climate hawk (person advocating aggressive steps regarding climate change)
Kropyvnytskyi, a city and raion of Ukraine.
Carpathia (the region of the Carpathian Mountains, in Central Europe)

karpatië.e: By surface analysis, Karpaten + -ië, but likely a loan: compare English Carpathia.

s.def: a superficial or budding fuckbuddy-type relationship which one considers promising and which may develop into something more serious
a person with whom one has such a relationship

kwarrel.e: Blend of kwaliteit (“kwaliteit”) + scharrel (“budding relationship, superficial relationship”); i.e., a high-quality scharrel.

kiddesj.e: Borrowed from Yiddish קידוש (kidesh), from Hebrew קידוש (kidúsh).

s.def: to be adept at, have extensive knowledge of
examples: Van algebra heeft hij geen kaas gegeten.

s.def: impossible, unrealistic or fantastic things; castles in the air
examples: De manager beloofde koeien met gouden horens in plaats van realistische doelen te stellen.

kaasjeskruid.e: From kaasje (“little cheese”) + -s- + kruid (“herb”). Named after the fruit of Malva sylvestris, which is said to resemble little cheeses.

s.def: plural of koekje van eigen deeg

examples: Onder de muizenverdelgende soorten noem ik den buizerd, den kerkuil, de roek en in streken, die niet zeer ver van zee zijn gelegen, de kobmeeuw.

kopjesbekermos.e: From kopje (“little cup”) + -s- + bekermos (“any of various lichens of genus Cladonia”).

examples: Zeg, komaan!, we laten ons er niet door van de wijs brengen.

s.def: a swoon out of adoration or admiration of some person

katzwijm.e: From kat (“cat”) + zwijm (“swoon, state of faded consciousness”).

examples: De kijkcijfers zijn gekelderd na het tweede seizoen.

s.def: programme that attracts a lot of viewers

examples: een kijkje nemen

kruipklokje.e: From kruipen (“to creep, crawl”) + klokje (“bellflower”); this bellflower species tends to grow in a crawling fashion and remains close to the ground, as is also reflected in the English name trailing bellflower.

keizerstad.e: From keizer (“emperor”) + stad (“city”), referring to the palace Charlemagne is said to have built there.

kaiserbroodje.e: Compound of German Kaiser (“emperor”) + broodje (“bread roll”), a partial calque of German Kaisersemmel.

s.def: to walk with a curved or forward leaning posture
to outrun, to escape from someone chasing you
snag, hitch, spanner in the works

kwalificatie.e: Borrowed from Middle French qualification, from Medieval Latin quālificātiō.

examples: Teken onderaan de pagina en klaar is Kees.

klaar is kees.e: Literally, “Kees has finished”, Kees being a male given name.

klassement.e: Borrowed from French classement, equivalent to klasse + -ment.

s.def: synonym of sierlijke vetmuur (“knotted pearlwort, Sagina nodosa”)

krielparnassia.e: From kriel (“something puny”) + parnassia (“parnassia, grass of Parnassus”); the plant superficially resembles a tiny parnassia.

kniesoor.e: From kniezen (“to whine; to piss and moan”) + oor; compare domoor and druiloor.

s.def: someone who skips or throws stones
tipper, dump truck (heavy truck with a hinged bed for unloading)
to tip (to empty out the contents of a container by tilting)

knetter-.e: From knettergek (“raving mad”), from knetteren (“to crackle”).

s.def: one who quacks or croaks, such as a duck or frog
creeping marshwort (Helosciadium repens syn. Apium repens)

examples: ‘Als je niet in het gareel loopt, dan word je kaltgestellt.’

kaltstellen.e: From German kaltstellen (“to put in a cold place; (metaphorically) to sideline”), from kalt (“cold”) + stellen (“to put, position”). First attested in 1994.

examples: De trainer vroeg tijdens de kennismakingsronde aan het begin van de basistraining aan iedere deelnemer met welke motivatie hij/zij aan de training begon.

klip-en-klaar.e: Borrowed from German klipp und klar; equivalent to klip + en + klaar.

s.def: Interjection affirming that the speaker will get something done.
examples:
a) Kan jij dat even doen?
b) Ja hoor, komt voor de bakker!

s.def: lousewort, any plant of genus Pedicularis

kartelblad.e: From kartelen (“to serrate; to be serrated”) or kartel (“serration”) + blad (“leaf”); species of this genus found in the Low Countries have serrated leaves (see picture).

s.def: a group of lakes by Kaag in South Holland, Netherlands

kagerplassen.e: Univerbation of Kager (“of or from Kaag”) + plassen (“pools, lakes”).

s.def: a crownvetch; any plant of genus Securigera

kroonkruid.e: Compound of kroon (“crown”) + kruid (“herb”); kroontjeskruid (“sun spurge”) was formed similarly.

s.def: to fellate to the point of orgasm

examples: Ze kandideerde zich voor de positie van burgermeester.
De partij wil hem niet kandideren.
Ik wil niet kandideren.

kandideren.e: From German kandidieren. By surface analysis, kandidaat + -eren.

s.def: voters considered to be easily manipulable; voters seen as a tool to get elected

kiesvee.e: From kiezen + vee. Compare German Wahlvieh.

s.def: a type of stonecrop, Phedimus hybridus

kwarktassen.e: 2010s coinage. From kwark (“quark (soft creamy cheese)”) + tassen (“bags”).

s.def: of, from or pertaining to Krimpen aan den IJssel
a day on which one eats leftovers instead of preparing a fresh meal

karmozijn.e: Early 16th century. From Middle French cramoisin, from Old French cramoisin, from Arabic قِرْمِز (qirmiz), from Classical Persian کرمست (kirmist).

s.def: synonym of soepschildpad (“green sea turtle (Chelonia mydas)”)

krapé.e: Likely of Amerindian origin, compare Old Tupi karambé. Cognate of Sranan Tongo krape.

examples: Het suikerriet is gedurende deszelfs groei aan weinige hindernissen blootgesteld, geheel misgewas kent men niet; wanneer men echter het land bemest, (dat slechts bij enkele planters, en dan nog zeer zeldzaam geschiedt,) willen er wel eens wormen in de rietstoelen en derzelver wortelen komen; ook is men vooral op de hooge landen met eene soort van kleine grijze ratten en veldmuizen geplaagd, alsmede door buffels of tapiers, kapoeá's of watervarkens, krabe dago's, houtluizen en mieren.
De Directeur van Landbouw, Veeteelt en Visserij maakt hierbij bekend dat: (...) de jacht op KAPOEWA gedurende het gehele jaar 1978 toegestaan is (...).

kapoewa.e: Borrowed from Sranan Tongo kapuwa, from Kari'na kapiwa and/or Lokono kapoa.

examples: stenen ketsen

ketsen.e: From Middle Dutch ketsen, borrowed from Old French cachier. Doublet of kaatsen.

examples: Coordinate term: groot avondrood

s.def: related to or based on a school class
examples: Op een Montessori-school werken de kinderen heel zelfstandig en minder klassikaal.

kotsbeu.e: Compound of kotsen (“to vomit”) + beu (“fed up”); the element kots- here has an intensifying charge, perhaps by analogy with kotsmisselijk (which is frequently used figuratively).

koekoekshommel.e: From koekoek (“cuckoo”) + -s- (“bumblebee”) + hommel. Like the cuckoo bird, bumblebees of genus Psithyrus engage in brood parasitism.

s.def: hairdo, haircut done at a regular frequency
examples: Het is weer eens tijd dat onze poedel een knipbeurt krijgt, want zo kan hij de wedstrijd nooit winnen.
Naast doe-het-zelven, bier drinken en een maandelijkse knipbeurt bij de kapper, gebeurt er niet veel.

s.def: kaboom (sound of an explosion or other loud noise)
a senecio; any plant of genus Senecio, or that was previously counted as part of that genus (such as the plants of genus Jacobaea)
Recreational drugs in powder form (typically cocaine or speed), delivered in a tiny envelope.
A major worry, a persisting source of vexation and stress.

kolenbrandersgeloof.e: From kolenbrander (“collier, charcoal burner”) + -s- + geloof, presumably a calque. Cf. German Köhlerglaube, French foi du charbonnier.

s.def: charcoal burner, collier (person who produces charcoal)
charcoal burner, a heater or brazier that burns on charcoal

kolenbrander.e: From kolen + brander. Cf. Middle Dutch colenberner.

s.def: A criminal or thuggish youth, particularly with a non-Western ethnic background.

kansparel.e: 2000s or 2010s coinage from kans (“chance, opportunity”) + parel (“pearl”), parodying real or imagined left-wing or liberal discourse apologetically depicting youths from socioeconomically disadvantaged and non-Western backgrounds as holding great but unrealized potential and promise.

koninginnenhapje.e: See the etymology of the corresponding lemma form.

s.def: elector, member of an electoral college, e.g. in US presidential elections (elected by the voters)
examples: Elke staat in de VS heeft een bepaald aantal kiesmensen die de president kiezen.
Na de verkiezingen worden de kiesmensen samengeroepen om de president te kiezen.

kiesmens.e: From the root of kiezen (“to choose, elect”) + mens (“person, human”).

s.def: synonym of kader (“cadre, core member of political organization”)

kwintessens.e: Borrowed from Middle Dutch quintessence, from Middle French quinte essence, itself borrowed from Medieval Latin quinta essentia. Influenced by German Quintessenz.

s.def: A type of sieve involving a chain.
A ballast cleaner or undercutter; a type of large machine involving steel chains, used to sieve track ballast.

kettinghor.e: From ketting (“chain”) + horde (“large sieve; wooden lattice; wattle screen; hurdle”). The word hor also exists, but with a specific meaning of "insect screen". Due to this semantic problem and the fact that the forms kettinghort and kettinghorde are (marginally) attested in the early 20th centur...

s.def: crowned pigeon (any bird of genus Goura)

examples: In mijn geboortestreek zijn mensen trots op hun klederdracht.
De klederdrachten van deze dorpen worden zelden gedragen.

klederdracht.e: From kleder(en) + dracht, klederen being the archaic antecedent of kleren (“clothing”). Compare German Tracht.

kapitaalkrachtig.e: From kapitaal + kracht + -ig, possibly a calque of German kapitalkräftig.

klonteren.e: From Middle Dutch clonteren. Equivalent to klont + -eren.

s.def: clumping; the process or result of forming lumps
alternative form of kontneuken (“to fuck anally”)

kontjeneuken.e: From kontje (“arse, bottom (diminutive)”) + neuken (“to fuck, have intercourse”).

knalerwt.e: Compound of knal or knallen + erwt.

kondig.e: From Middle Dutch condich. By surface analysis, kond + -ig.

examples: 'Tini, ga nou niet al die Duitsers op je eentje doodmaken,' had zijn neef tegen hem gezegd. 'Als ik er drie koud maak voor ze mij koud maken heb ik daar geen probleem mee,' had Tini in zijn dronkenschap geantwoord.

koud maken.e: Literally make cold. Refers to the drop in body temperature after dying.

s.def: second-growth forest; thicket, shrubland, brushwood (vegetation that regenerates after the clearing of primary forests)

kapoeweri.e: From Sranan Tongo kapuweri, from Portuguese capoeira (“glade; clearing; area of land devoid of trees”), possibly from Old Tupi kopûera, Old Tupi ka'apûera, or Old Tupi ka'apaũ. Doublet of capoeira.

s.def: gnarl, knot (hard protuberance, especially on a tree)

knoest.e: From Middle Dutch cnoest. Compare knuist, German Knust.

knoestig.e: From Middle Dutch cnoestich. By surface analysis, knoest + -ig.

s.def: A blob or splash of a thick liquid.
examples: Er zit een klodder pindakaas op je broek.

klodder.e: Byform of klad, possibly influenced by words like flodder, lodderig, and modder.

s.def: To work in a careless and unprofessional manner, messing around.
examples: 'Wat is dat voor een visbestek waarmee je daar staat te klodderen?' 'Ik klodder niet!' riep heer Ollie verontwaardigd uit. 'En dit zijn pas échte gereedschappen voor een tere kunstbeeldhouwer, die er mee kan timmeren zonder zijn gevoelige handen te bezeren?'

s.def: acronym of Koninklijke Nederlandse Akademie van Wetenschappen (“Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences”)
to knock out the way, also figuratively

koevogel.e: Compound of koe + vogel, possibly a calque.

s.def: A children's chair with a built-in chamber pot.

klein hoefblad.e: Literally, “small hoof-leaf”, to be contrasted with groot hoefblad (“common butterbur”).

kwee.e:
From Middle Dutch quede, from Old Dutch [Term?], from Proto-West Germanic [Term?], from Latin *quidōnea (see cotōneum, cydōnium), from Ancient Greek κυδώνιον (kudṓnion).
The n seen in Latin and Greek was lost at some point after the Germanic borrowing from Latin, likely due to its re-interpretati...

kruim.e: From Middle Dutch crume, from Old Dutch *krūmo, from Proto-West Germanic *krūmō, from Proto-Germanic *krūmô.

s.def: initialism of Kamer van Koophandel en Fabrieken (“chamber of commerce”)
to laugh one's head off, die laughing
examples: De peuter lachte zich kapot om de volwassenen.

kapotlachen.e: Compound of kapot (“broken, defective”) + lachen (“to laugh”).

koningskaars.e: From koning (“king”) + -s- + kaars (“candle”); kaars is used for some species of the genus Verbascum (mullein), for which toorts is usually used.

s.def: to play kienen, a variant of bingo

kienen.e: From the interjection kien used in the game, with verbal suffix -en. The interjection kien derives from French quine.

kienspel.e: Compound of kienen (“to play kienen”) + spel (“game”).

s.def: A holiday in honor of Saint Nicholas celebrated on the island of Schiermonnikoog.
One who is in charge of a kitchen, such as a (head) chef.

keukenmeester.e: From Middle Dutch cokenmeester. By surface analysis, keuken (“kitchen”) + meester (“master”).

s.def: the act or process of crumbling

kruimelen.e: From kruimen + -elen and/or kruimel + -en. Compare English crumble.

kruimen.e: From Middle Dutch crumen. By surface analysis, kruim (“crumb”) + -en.

koopmanschap.e: From Middle Dutch coopmanscap, copenschap, possibly inherited from Proto-West Germanic [Term?]; compare Middle High German koufmanschaft (German Kaufmannschaft), Middle Low German kōpmanschop, English chapmanship, Old Frisian kapenskip (West Frisian keapmanskip). By surface analysis, koopman (“me...

s.def: Nickname for an inhabitant of Tilburg, particularly during Carnival.

kruikenzeiker.e: From kruik (“jug”) + -en- + zeiken (“to piss”) + -er; apparently a reference to the textile industry, as urine was historically used in the production of textile and Tilburg was known for its textile industry.

s.def: A child who works as an influencer.
Long cloth from the shoulders to the feet.

keurs.e:
From Middle Dutch kuerse, probably borrowed via Middle High German kürsen (“fur garment”), from Old High German kursina (“fur”), from Old East Slavic кꙋрьзно (kurĭzno), from Proto-Slavic *kъrzьno (“fur coat”) (whence Russian корзно́ (korznó, “coat with fur trimming”)).
Compare Middle Low German k...

s.def: A tree that can be climbed with ease, particularly as part of children's play.
examples: De kinderen probeerden zo hoog mogelijk in de klimboom te klauteren.

Omwonenden van het park hebben er bij de gemeente op aangedrongen een speelgelegenheid voor de kinderen te scheppen, omdat in de omgeving vrijwel geen speelmogelijkheden aanwezig zijn. De geplande voorziening omvat en^([sic]) houten wigwam een klauterwerktuig en een klimboom.

koewarm.e: Compound of koe (“cow”) + warm (“warm”); possibly inherited from a Proto-West Germanic *kūwarm, considering Old English cūwearm and German kuhwarm.

klassebak.e: Compound of klasse (“of high quality”) + bak (“car”).

kristalliseren.e: Borrowed from French cristalliser. By surface analysis, kristal + -iseren.

kwinkslag.e: From Middle Dutch quincslach. By surface analysis, kwink + slag.

s.def: to cannibalise (eat (parts of) another of one's own species)
examples: Daar de mannetjes 95% van hun tijd op het nest zitten en dus weinig tijd hebben om buitenshuis te fourageren, kannibaliseren ze eitjes uit hun eigen nest: gemideld ^([sic – meaning gemiddeld]) 36 per dag.

s.def: to cannibalise (remove parts of (a machine, etc) for use in other similar machines)
examples: Van die Neptunes vliegt er nog maar een. De rest is zoals het in vaktermen heet gekannibaliseerd; onderdelen die niet meer te krijgen zijn zijn er vanaf gesloopt om andere toestellen in de lucht te houden.

s.def: to cannibalise (reduce sales or market share (for one of one's own products) by introducing another)
examples: Is de kans niet groot dat V8 zusterzenders SBS 6 en Net5 zal kannibaliseren, er kijkers zal wegtrekken?

klanttevredenheidsonderzoek.e: Compound of klanttevredenheid (“customer satisfaction”) + -s- + onderzoek (“research, survey”).

examples: De uitnodiging was te kort dag om me normaal voor te kunnen bereiden.

kransmuur.e: Compound of krans (“wreath”) + muur (“any of various small plants of the Caryophyllaceae family”).

kalmoes.e: Borrowed from Latin calamus, from Ancient Greek κάλαμος (kálamos). Doublet of halm.

s.def: to handle a liquid messily; to daub, to stain

kliederen.e: A variant of kledderen, itself from kladderen, a frequentative of kladden.

s.def: synonym of Anna Paulownaboom (“princess tree (Paulownia tomentosa)”)

keizersboom.e: Compound of keizer (“emperor”) + -s- + boom (“tree”). Compare English empress tree.

s.def: hallmark (official marking made by a trusted party, usually an assay office, on items made of precious metals)
examples: Als het gehalte goed is, krijgt het voorwerp pneumatisch een keurteken; […]
Ook deze landen kennen een wettelijk verplicht systeem van een onafhankelijke keuring vooraf en het laten aanbrengen van een keurteken.
Door het Rijkswaarborgkantoor, te Utrecht wordt op beide zilverwerken het Rijkswaarborgmerk voor massief zilver van het gehalte 835/1000 gestempeld, alsmede ook het Kantoorteken en het Jaarmerk.

krulvaren.e: From krullen (“to curl”) or krul (“curl”) + varen (“fern”).

s.def: to get ready or prepare something by putting it in a place
examples: Ik had dat boek nog wel klaargelegd, maar toch vergeten.

s.def: collective name for several species of shrubs and trees in the family Vochysiaceae, notably Vochysia surinamensis

kwari.e: Borrowed from Sranan Tongo kwari, from Kari'na kuwari.

examples: Ach kassian, het is voorbij / kassian, het is voorbij / Den Haag, Den Haag / de weduwe van Indië ben jij

s.def: celeriac, celery root (Apium graveolens var. rapaceum, a vegetable)
Any sport in which strength is of paramount importance, as opposed to sports centering around e.g. endurance.
examples: Coordinate term: duursport

krachtsport.e: Compound of kracht (“power, strength”) + sport (“sport”). Compare German Kraftsport.

koffieboonspin.e: Compound of koffieboon (“coffee bean”) + spin (“spider”), referring to the resemblance of the abdomen to a coffee bean.

komkommerspin.e: Compound of komkommer (“cucumber”) + spin (“spider”), referring to the bright green ("cucumber") color of the abdomen of spiders of this genus.

kameleonspin.e: Compound of kameleon (“chameleon”) + spin (“spider”); spiders of this species can change color like a chameleon, although they do so much more slowly and are mostly restricted to white and yellow.

s.def: A cobweb spider; any spider of the family Theridiidae.
electable (person who is able or fit to be elected to office)

kukelen.e: From Middle Dutch kokelen (“to trick, betray”). Perhaps related to goochelen.

examples: Hij was kind aan huis bij mijn grootvader.

s.def: royal bolete (Butyriboletus regius syn. Boletus regius)
king trumpet mushroom, king oyster mushroom (Pleurotus eryngii)

kruisdisteloesterzwam.e: Compound of kruisdistel (“eryngium”) + oesterzwam (“oyster mushroom”), so named (compare its taxonomic name Pleurotus eryngii) because it grows in association with plants of genus Eryngium, that is, eryngos and sea hollies.

s.def: eryngium, eryngo, sea holly (plant of genus Eryngium)
field eryngo, Watling Street thistle (Eryngium campestre)

kruisdistel.e: Compound of kruis (“cross”) + distel (“thistle”). The naming is unclear; there appears to be no resemblance to a cross in Eryngium campestre or other Eryngium species in the Low Countries.

kommer en kwel.e: Coined by Dutch comic strip author Maarten Toonder. Literally, “grief and torment”

kwetteren.e: From earlier queteren, from Middle Dutch *queteren (attested in Middle Dutch queteringhe (“chattering, chit-chat”)). Cognate with West Frisian kwitterje, kwetterje (“to chatter”), Saterland Frisian kwetelje, kwättelje (“to jabber”), German Low German queteln (“to chatter, jabber”), German quassel...

kletskont.e: Compound of kletsen (“to chat”) + kont (“butt, ass”).

kwebbelkont.e: Compound of kwebbelen (“to chatter”) + kont (“butt, ass”).

s.def: a type of early modern cannon

kartouw.e: From Middle Dutch cortouwe (late 15th century), from Middle French courtault (French courtaud). Compare German Kartaune.

s.def: to not interest or bother someone at all
examples: Dat laat me koud.
De crisis in het buurland liet hem koud.

kretoloog.e: By surface analysis, kreet + -loog, but probably a formation based on more common kretologie.

kamperfoelie.e: From earlier caprifolie, from Latin caprifolium.

s.def: Plagioscion surinamensis (a species of freshwater ray-finned fish)
examples: Ma Lien trok zich hierop terug in de keuken en begon met het schoonmaken van koebi. Het huis raakte doortrokken van de vislucht. Schubben sprongen op de keukenvloer.

koebi.e: From Sranan Tongo kubi, possibly from Twi Akan koobi.

s.def: an ugly or unlikable face or head
examples: Wat een lelijke kutkop heb jij, zeg.

kajoe.e: Borrowed from Malay kayu (“wood”). Clipping of tukang kayu.

s.def: curb, kerb: The raised edge between the pavement and the roadway, typically made of concrete though originally consisting of a line of kerbstones

kantsteen.e: Compound of kant + steen. Cognate of Danish kantsten c, German Kantstein m.

keboen.e: Borrowed from Malay kebun (“garden”), a clipping of tukang kebun (“gardener”).

kramassen.e: Adapted borrowing of Malay keramas (“to wash hair”) + -en.

s.def: cope (long, loose cloak worn by a priest, deacon, or bishop when presiding over a ceremony other than the Mass)

klapperboom.e: Compound of klapper (“coconut”) + boom (“tree”); the former is derived from Malay kelapa.

s.def: a layered butter cake with fruit jam between the cake layers from Indo-Dutch origin
to make a crunching, creaking, grinding noise
examples: De sneeuw knerpte onder haar laarzen.

knerpen.e: Possibly a blend of knarsen + snerpen and/or onomatopoeic.

s.def: to grind or gnash one's teeth
header: the act of hitting the ball with the head.

examples: De kattenkrabziekte ontstaat door een krab of beet van een kat die besmet is met de Bartonella henselae-bacterie.

koelieboontjes.e: Compound of koelie (“coolie”) + boontjes (“little beans”), referring to the fact that this plant was traditionally almost exclusively cultivated by Indo-Surinamese farmers. Cognate of Sranan Tongo kulibonki.

kraagtrap.e: From kraag (“frill”) + trap (“bustard”). The bird has more prominent frill feathers than other bustards.

kritische prestatie-indicator.e: Calque of English key performance indicator.

s.def: baby blues experienced shortly after childbirth

kaalslaan.e: Calque of German kahlschlagen. By surface analysis, kaal + slaan.

s.def: to make bald by cutting with scissors
to completely clear by plucking or picking
to clear or strip completely by eating
the state or condition of having a bald head; baldness
diminutive of kaal; bare, featureless, plain
team set up to retrieve stolen goods from criminals
to throw away, dump, empty a container by tipping it
